title: I'm a senior citizen or have a disability
subtitle: Special facilities and home voting options

intro.title: ECI provides several facilities for you.
intro.body: Whether you'd prefer to vote at home, at the booth with assistance, or anywhere in between — there's a process for it.

choices.label: Person with 40%+ disability
choices.sublabel: Eligible for home voting & priority access

choices.label: Senior under 85, or PwD with under 40%

homeVote.title: A polling team will come to you.
homeVote.body:
On a designated day before the main election, a polling team visits eligible voters' homes with a ballot. You vote in privacy. The sealed ballot is collected and counted on counting day.

The key is to **apply early** — usually within the first few days after election notification.

applyHomeVote.title: File Form 12D with your ERO.
applyHomeVote.body: Download Form 12D from the ECI portal. Submit it to your Electoral Registration Officer (ERO) within the deadline. PwD applicants attach their disability certificate.

boothPriority.title: You don't have to stand in line.
boothPriority.body:
All polling booths must:
- Have **wheelchair access ramps**
- Allow **priority entry** for senior and PwD voters (skip the queue)
- Permit a **companion** (18+) inside the voting compartment
- Provide **Braille** ballot for visually impaired voters

You can also request transport via the Saksham app.

saksham.title: Get personalized assistance.
saksham.body:
ECI's **Saksham app** is built for senior and PwD voters. Use it to:
- Request transport to your booth
- Request a wheelchair on-site
- Apply for home voting
- Find the most accessible booth near you

complete.title: Your vote, your way.
complete.summary: Whether at home or at the booth, you're entitled to a smooth voting experience. Don't hesitate to ask the Presiding Officer for assistance — it's their job.
